evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Core SDK usage (posthog-js init, PostHogProvider, useFeatureFlagEnabled, usePostHog, posthog-python Posthog() with capture/identify) is internally consistent and matches typical PostHog patterns. However, identify_context/new_context are non-standard methods for posthog-python (standard is capture(distinct_id, event, ...) and identify(distinct_id, ...)); this risks AttributeError. No explicit version pin.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse. Frontend init code looks runnable. Backend uses identify_context/new_context which may not exist in posthog-python — would cause runtime error. Circular import risk between app/main.py and app/routes/auth.py (auth imports posthog_client from main, but main needs to include router). No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/brew/sudo).
evidence.completeness: (a) Key acquisition mentioned with URL but light on menu navigation; (b) install bash blocks present; (c) env var template present and names match usage (VITE_POSTHOG_PROJECT_TOKEN, POSTHOG_API_KEY); (d) backend code present; (e) frontend code present; (f) test commands/examples present but no single canonical run command. Some duplicate env blocks.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple official posthog.com/docs and fastapi.tiangolo.com URLs cited inline near relevant code sections. No fabricated URLs detected.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; applied long-tail handling. identify_context/new_context appear to be hallucinated or very-new API surface for posthog-python — drops correctness to 3. Otherwise internally consistent.
